prose:
Method-provenance block
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap of pyridine, energies of frontier orbitals; units eV.
Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP, no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF backend.
Geometry provenance: single-point calculation on user-supplied geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz); no geometry optimization (DFT//user-XYZ).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet).
Software: PySCF (via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill; version as packaged with chemkit runtime).
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 10 cycles; no engine warnings were reported. Integrity check status: ok, trustworthy = true. No warnings reported.

Key results
HOMO energy  −7.2525992 eV
LUMO energy  −1.112786 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap 6.1398131 eV

Reproducibility
Command executed (from cli_invocation):
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --accept-defaults --charge 0 --mult 1 --stdout json /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp_ttz6a_3/live.json
Output JSON: pyridine_frontier_standard_dft.json (see path above).
A live PySCF log was streamed during the run to the engine-generated .out file (path emitted at launch in the session transcript); it contains full SCF details.

The headline HOMO–LUMO gap is therefore:
6.1398131 eV.
